subject: Project Management
title: Extreme Programming Emerges
description: A fast breakdown of Extreme Programming Emerges for builders.

hook.text: In 1996, Kent Beck joined a struggling Chrysler payroll project and tried something radical.

buildup.text: He paired programmers together, wrote tests before code, and released software every two weeks.

discovery.text: He called it Extreme Programming — XP — because it took good practices to their logical extreme.

twist.text: Critics called it reckless. No upfront design? Testing first? Pair programming? Madness.

climax.text: XP teams delivered faster and with fewer bugs. The results silenced most skeptics.

punchline.text: The 'reckless' method worked better than the careful one.
